title: Guy's and St Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust
text:
Reference: IC-474974-J0B9 
 1 
Freedom of Information Act 2000 (FOIA) 
Decision notice 
 
 
Date: 12 March 2026 
  
Public Authority: Guy's and St Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust 
Address: St Thomas’ Hospital 
Westminster Bridge Road 
London 
SE1 7EH 
 
1. Request: 
The complainant requested the following information from Guy's and St 
Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust on 18 December 2025: 
 
“I am writing to request the following information under the Freedom of 
Information Act 2000. This request relates solely to non-personal, 
organisational, system-level and policy information. 
Unless otherwise stated, the information requested applies to the period 1 
January 2023 to 31 December 2024. Where a more specific period is 
identified, please apply that period only. 
 
CALL-HANDLING SYSTEMS 
1. What telephone or call-handling systems are used by: 
a) the Patient Advice and Liaison Service (PALS), 
b) the Early Pregnancy and Acute Gynaecology Unit (EPAGU), 
c) the Emergency Department (ED), and 
d) the Trust’s Complaints Department. 
2. Whether these systems automatically record any of the following call 
metadata: 
a) incoming call date and time, 
b) outgoing call date and time, 
c) call duration, 

Reference: IC-474974-J0B9 
 2 
d) missed or unanswered calls, 
e) voicemail messages left, 
f) call attempt status. 
3. Whether outbound call attempts made by staff within PALS, EPAGU, 
ED or the Complaints Department are automatically logged by the 
system, or require manual recording. 
 
CALL LOGS AND RECORD-KEEPING 
4. Whether staff are required or encouraged to manually record 
telephone contacts with patients, and if so: 
a) where such records are held, and 
b) whether such records form part of the official record. 
5. Whether the absence of a call log or record is treated as confirmation 
that a call did not occur. 
6. The retention period for: 
a) call metadata, and 
b) manual call logs or notes. 
INCOMING CALL MANAGEMENT 
7. How incoming calls are managed by PALS, EPAGU, ED and the 
Complaints Department. 
8. Whether incoming calls: 
a) are queued, 
b) diverted to voicemail, 
c) receive recorded messages, or 
d) are terminated if unanswered. 
9. Who answers incoming calls and whether triage or prioritisation is 
used. 
10. Procedures followed when calls cannot be answered, including    
redirection. 
11. Whether unanswered, abandoned calls or peak volumes are 
monitored and reviewed. 
USE OF CALL RECORDS IN COMPLAINTS 
12. Whether call records are relied upon in PALS enquiries or formal 
complaints. 

Reference: IC-474974-J0B9 
 3 
13. Any guidance or SOPs governing logging and reliance on call records. 
EPAGU TELEPHONE SERVICE AVAILABILITY 
Relevant period: 1 October 2023 to 31 January 2024 
14. Whether EPAGU operated as voicemail-only during this period. 
15. Details of any voicemail-only periods. 
16. Whether authorisation was required and documented. 
17. Reasons recorded for voicemail-only operation. 
18. Alternative access arrangements communicated. 
19. Whether such periods were logged or reviewed. 
20. Policies governing suspension or modification of EPAGU telephone 
access. 
VERIFICATION, AUDIT AND RELIABILITY 
21. Whether call systems generate audit trails. 
22. Whether call data can be altered and whether changes are logged. 
23. Safeguards ensuring accuracy and integrity of call logs. 
24. Known limitations or reliability issues. 
25. Instances of missing or incomplete call logs. 
VERIFICATION IN COMPLAINT INVESTIGATIONS 
26. Steps taken to verify call records. 
27. Requirement to consider system limitations. 
28. Guidance or training on evaluating call evidence. 
CONTEMPORANEOUS RECORDS 
29. Whether call-backs are recorded in other systems. 
30. Whether investigators cross-check records. 
 

Reference: IC-474974-J0B9 
 4 
TELEPHONE BILLING DATA 
31. Whether itemised billing data is held. 
32. What information billing data contains. 
33. How billing data is used. 
34. Whether billing data is used for cross-checking. 
35. Retention period for billing data. 
36. Policies governing billing data use.” 
2. Commissioner’s Decision:  
A public authority will breach section 10 of FOIA if it fails to respond to a 
request within 20 working days. Based on evidence available to the 
Commissioner, by the date of this notice the public authority has not issued 
a substantive response to this request. Therefore the Commissioner finds a 
breach of section 10. 
3. Steps: 
The public authority must provide a substantive response to the request in 
accordance with its obligations under FOIA.   
The public authority must take this step within 30 calendar days of the 
date of this decision notice. Failure to comply may result in the 
Commissioner making written certification of this fact to the High Court 
pursuant to section 54 of FOIA and may be dealt with as a contempt of 
court. 
4. Right of Appeal: 
Either party has the right to appeal against this decision notice to the First-
tier Tribunal (Information Rights). Information about the appeals process 
may be obtained from: 
First-tier Tribunal (Information Rights) 
General Regulatory Chamber 
PO Box 11230 
Leicester 
LE1 8FQ 
 
Tel: 0203 936 8963 

Reference: IC-474974-J0B9 
 5 
 
 
Information Commissioner 
Wycliffe House  
Water Lane  
Wilmslow  
Cheshire  
SK9 5AF  
 
Fax: 0870 739 5836 
Email: grc@justice.gov.uk  
Website: www.justice.gov.uk/tribunals/general-regulatory-chamber  
Any Notice of Appeal should be served on the Tribunal within 28 (calendar) 
days of the date on which this decision notice is sent.
description: The complainant requested information from Guy's and St Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust. The Commissioner’s decision is that the public authority failed to respond to the request within 20 working days and has therefore breached section 10 of FOIA. The public authority must provide a substantive response to the request in accordance with its obligations under FOIA.
